frameworks.name: executive function (inhibitory control, working memory, cognitive flexibility)
frameworks.one_line: Three core executive components are probed before and after stimulation.

frameworks.name: transcranial direct current stimulation (HD-tDCS)
frameworks.one_line: Anodal high-definition tDCS over left DLPFC as neuromodulatory intervention.

frameworks.name: functional near-infrared spectroscopy (fNIRS) hemodynamics
frameworks.one_line: Bilateral DLPFC HbO concentration tracks cortical activation during executive tasks.

core_question: Do repeated anodal HD-tDCS sessions over left DLPFC alter the three core executive functions and their associated DLPFC activation in healthy adults?
key_assumptions: DLPFC is the cortical substrate of executive function
Repeated anodal stimulation produces cumulative neuromodulatory effects beyond single-session tDCS
fNIRS HbO concentration indexes task-related DLPFC activation
manipulations: Number of repeated stimulation sessions (nine sessions)
Executive function probe (inhibitory control, working memory, cognitive flexibility)
key_findings: Cognitive flexibility improved more in the anodal group than in the sham group
Stroop-related decrease in DLPFC HbO concentration appeared in the anodal but not the sham group
interpretations: Repeated anodal HD-tDCS over left DLPFC can significantly promote cognitive flexibility
